name: LoanMax Title Loans
description_short: Founded in 1990, LoanMax Title Loans offers auto title loans up to $10,000 across multiple states, requiring only a vehicle, photo ID, and clear title.
description_long:
LoanMax Title Loans was founded in 1990 in Jonesboro, Georgia, and has operated for over three decades as a storefront auto title lender. The company is licensed and regulated across multiple states including Virginia, Delaware, South Carolina, New Mexico, Nevada, and Michigan. Its corporate complaint address is listed in Alpharetta, GA, and its Michigan locations are regulated by the Michigan Department of Insurance and Financial Services.

LoanMax provides cash loans secured by the equity in a borrower's vehicle — commonly called car title loans or auto title loans. Loan amounts go up to $10,000, subject to vehicle evaluation and the borrower's ability to repay. The application process is in-store and requires three items: the vehicle itself, a government-issued photo ID, and a clear (lien-free) vehicle title. Accepted repayment methods include cash, check, debit card, and Western Union. The Southgate, Michigan branch is open Monday through Friday 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM and Saturday 9:00 AM to 2:00 PM.

What distinguishes LoanMax is its multi-state regulatory transparency — the website includes state-specific licensing disclosures, consumer rights pamphlets, rate schedules, and complaint contact information for each regulated state. Michigan customers are directed to the state's Department of Insurance and Financial Services for escalation. Multiple Michigan branches are listed nearby (Taylor, Garden City, Detroit, Center Line), indicating regional density. The company also accepts Western Union payments, which is unusual and may benefit customers without bank accounts.

Honest assessment: title loans are high-cost, short-term financial instruments. LoanMax's own Nevada disclosure states these loans 'should be used for short-term financial needs only and not as a long-term financial solution,' and directs customers with credit difficulties to seek credit counseling beforehand. The loan is secured by your vehicle title, meaning failure to repay can result in repossession. LoanMax does not publish its interest rates or APRs on the website, which makes cost comparison difficult before visiting a branch. This product is best treated as a last resort for borrowers who own their vehicle outright and face a genuine short-term cash emergency.
pros: Founded in 1990 — over 35 years of operating history as a title lender
Loans up to $10,000 based on vehicle equity, higher ceiling than most payday lenders
Minimal requirements: vehicle, photo ID, and clear title only — no credit check mentioned
Accepts multiple payment methods including Western Union, useful for unbanked customers
Saturday hours available (9:00 AM – 2:00 PM) for borrowers who can't visit on weekdays
State-specific regulatory disclosures published on the website for VA, DE, SC, NM, NV, and MI
Multiple Michigan branch locations (Southgate, Taylor, Garden City, Detroit, Center Line)
cons: Interest rates and APRs are not disclosed anywhere on the website — cost is opaque before visiting
Loan is secured by your vehicle title — repossession risk if you default
Nevada disclosure explicitly warns against using title loans as a long-term financial solution
In-store only process with no visible online application or pre-qualification tool
Closed Sundays; Saturday hours end at 2:00 PM, limiting access for working borrowers
best_for: Vehicle owners with a clear (lien-free) title who need fast cash and cannot qualify for traditional credit
Borrowers facing a short-term emergency (medical, utility, rent) who can repay within weeks
Michigan residents near one of their branch locations who need same-visit cash disbursement
Unbanked or underbanked individuals who need to repay via cash, check, or Western Union
diagnosis: LoanMax Title Loans suits vehicle owners with a clear title who need up to $10,000 quickly and have exhausted lower-cost options such as credit union loans or personal loans. The main caveat is that interest rates are not disclosed online, the loan is secured by your vehicle (repossession risk is real), and the company itself warns in its Nevada disclosure that title loans are not a long-term financial solution.
services: Car title loans (auto title loans) up to $10,000
In-store vehicle equity evaluation for loan sizing
Same-visit cash disbursement at branch locations
Online payment portal (Pay Online via My Account)
Multi-state licensed lending (VA, DE, SC, NM, NV, MI)
Walk-in loan services with no appointment required
State-specific consumer rights and licensing disclosures
